name: pyFFTW (Python Wrapper for FFTW)

auth.notes: Local library; requires FFTW3 C library

performance.notes: 2-10x faster than NumPy FFT for large arrays; FFTW wisdom for repeated transforms

agent_readiness.notes: Python wrapper for FFTW3 - fastest FFT library. import pyfftw. pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft.fft(a). pyfftw.interfaces.scipy_fft.rfft2(a). Drop-in: import pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft as np_fft; np_fft.fft(a). Plan: a = pyfftw.empty_aligned(1024, dtype='complex128'); fft = pyfftw.FFTW(a, pyfftw.empty_aligned(1024)). Wisdom: pyfftw.export_wisdom(); pyfftw.import_wisdom(). Threads: FFTW(a, b, threads=4). 2D/ND transforms. Real-to-complex (rfft). FFTW wisdom for repeated same-size transforms.

gotchas: Requires FFTW3 C library — not pure Python install
First call with wisdom off is slow — plan creation overhead
Memory alignment requirements (empty_aligned) for maximum speed
Drop-in interface is slower than optimized FFTW class
best_when: Repeated large FFTs where planning pays off (imaging, signal processing)
When NumPy FFT is a bottleneck (2-10x speedup possible)
Multi-threaded FFT with FFTW's OpenMP parallelism
Drop-in numpy.fft replacement with minimal code changes
avoid_when: One-time FFTs where planning overhead isn't justified
When NumPy FFT speed is sufficient
summary: pyFFTW wraps FFTW3 for 2-10x faster FFTs than NumPy. pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft.fft() drop-in. FFTW(a, b, threads=4) for optimized plans. Wisdom export/import for repeated same-size transforms. BSD-3 by Henry Gomersall. Best when FFT is a bottleneck in signal/image processing. Requires FFTW3 C library. Plan creation adds startup overhead — amortized over many transforms.
